courses.title: Introduction to Biology: Principles of Ecology & Evolution

notes: Guarantee your admission to UC Davis! The UC Davis Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a written agreement ensuring your admission to UC Davis from any California Community College. Please visit the TAG webpage for more information!

Transfer students are strongly encouraged to work closely with the California Community College counselors for transfer preparation to UC Davis. Transfer students may also contact a UC Davis transfer advisor for assistance. The below agreements should be utilized in conjunction with professional advising.
Please note that the Department Agreements as published on ASSIST are also important documents and should be referenced in conjunction with the major agreements as needed.

Group 7: In addition to the below major preparation requirements, students must also choose two courses satisfying Social Sciences General Education requirement; which may count toward both GE and the major. Please contact an advisor or visit the major for more information.
Please refer to the Transfer Admissions Requirements for information on admissions to this major.

Landscape architecture is the planning and design of land areas where human use requires adaptation or conservation of the environment. Students who study landscape architecture are concerned about the welfare of the environment and the people who use and shape it. They are capable of solving physical problems and are able to visualize and think in terms of spaces and three-dimensional concepts. The program is fully accredited by the Landscape Architecture Accreditation Board (LAAB) which is the only organization professionally sanctioned to grant landscape architectural accreditations in the United States. The program was last reviewed in 2018. The curriculum balances creativity and visual and spatial skills with technological expertise and a thorough background in physical, natural, and social sciences. Students develop proficiency at problem solving relating to design of parks, public spaces, energy-efficient neighborhoods, land reclamation projects, city and regional planning, and landscape planning for wilderness and scenic regions, coastal and riparian environments, and other sensitive land areas. The program stresses a process-oriented approach to design and emphasizes environmental and community values.

Graduates may find jobs in private landscape architectural firms or public agencies, non-profit organizations, and corporations employing landscape architects. The landscape architecture major provides the student with excellent preparation for graduate school or career development in a wide range of environmental and design-related fields.

Students who wish to major in Landscape Architecture should apply to Pre-Landscape Architecture . This major has no specific major course requirements, but students who wish to apply for a Transfer Admission Guarantee in this major must have an overall 3.20 GPA. After enrolling at UC Davis, pre-landscape architecture majors will be required to submit a separate portfolio application to the Landscape Architecture department in February. Contact the Landscape Architecture Advising Office at 530-754-8628 or visit humanecology.ucdavis.edu for details about the portfolio application.
